title: ～とあれば
structure: Verb-casual + とあれば / い-Adjective + とあれば / な-Adjective + だとあれば / Noun + だとあれば
structureEn: Verb-casual + とあれば / い-Adjective + とあれば / な-Adjective + だとあれば / Noun + だとあれば
explanation: Expresses a condition; 'if', 'when', 'since'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とあれば grammar point is used to express a condition or premise in which a certain outcome or action naturally follows. It can be translated as 'if,' 'when,' or 'since,' depending on context. It emphasizes that under this specific condition, a certain response is expected.

title: ～といえども
titleEn: Even if it is said that; Even though; Although.
structure: Noun + といえども / Verb-plain form + といえども / Adjective + といえども
structureEn: Noun + といえども / Verb-plain form + といえども / Adjective + といえども
explanation: Even if it is said that; Even though; Although. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～といえども grammar point is used to show a contrast or contradiction. It is similar to 'even though' or 'although' in English. It can be used with nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc., to emphasize that the reality is different from the assumption or label.

title: ～といったらありはしない
structure: Noun + といったらありはしない / な-Adjective + といったらありはしない
structureEn: Noun + といったらありはしない / な-Adjective + といったらありはしない
explanation: Express extreme exclamation; 'extremely', 'unbelievable', 'unsurpassed'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～といったらありはしない grammar point is used to express an extreme degree of something, often surprise or admiration. It can be loosely translated as 'unbelievably...' or 'incredibly...'.

title: ～といったらありゃしない
titleEn: Emphasizes that something is to the extreme; 'you have no idea how...', 'it's incredibly...'.
structure: Verb-casual + といったらありゃしない / い-Adjective + といったらありゃしない / な-Adjective + だ + といったらありゃしない / Noun + だ + といったらありゃしない
structureEn: Verb-casual + といったらありゃしない / い-Adjective + といったらありゃしない / な-Adjective + だ + といったらありゃしない / Noun + だ + といったらありゃしない
explanation: Emphasizes that something is to the extreme; 'you have no idea how...', 'it's incredibly...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～といったらありゃしない grammar point is an idiomatic expression used to emphasize that something is extremely or incredibly a certain way—often beyond words. It can be loosely rendered as 'you wouldn't believe how...' or 'it's indescribably...'.

title: ～といったらない
titleEn: Expresses the extreme of something; 'nothing can be more ... than this'.
structure: い-Adjective + といったらない / な-Adjective + だといったらない / Noun + だといったらない
structureEn: い-Adjective + といったらない / な-Adjective + だといったらない / Noun + だといったらない
explanation: Expresses the extreme of something; 'nothing can be more ... than this'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～といったらない grammar point is used to emphasize the high degree or intensity of an action or state. It can be translated as “nothing can be more ... than this” or “it’s extremely...”. It conveys that something is so intense or extreme that it cannot be adequately described.

title: ～ときている
titleEn: Highlights a situation or condition that is serious/extreme, often with a negative nuance.
structure: Verb-casual / い-Adjective / な-Adjective（＋だ） / Noun（＋だ） + ときている
structureEn: Verb-casual / い-Adjective / な-Adjective（＋だ） / Noun（＋だ） + ときている
explanation: Highlights a situation or condition that is serious/extreme, often with a negative nuance. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ときている is used to emphasize that some circumstance is particularly notable—often negatively. It implies, “Given that things are so ~, it’s no wonder that...” or “Because the situation is (too) ~, such-and-such follows.” It is relatively informal and more common in spoken language.

title: ～ところを
titleEn: Indicates a contrasting or inconvenient timing; 'just when...', 'even though...'.
structure: Verb-ている + ところを / Noun + の + ところを / い-Adjective + ところを / な-Adjective + な + ところを
structureEn: Verb-ている + ところを / Noun + の + ところを / い-Adjective + ところを / な-Adjective + な + ところを
explanation: Indicates a contrasting or inconvenient timing; 'just when...', 'even though...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point ～ところを is often used when something happens (or one acts) at a certain moment/condition that makes the timing inconvenient or contrary to what is expected. It can imply “in spite of the fact that...” or “just when...” depending on context.

title: ～とされる
titleEn: Indicates a general belief or widely accepted view; 'is considered', 'is seen as', 'is said to be'.
structure: Verb-casual + とされる / い-Adjective + とされる / な-Adjective + だとされる / Noun + だとされる
structureEn: Verb-casual + とされる / い-Adjective + とされる / な-Adjective + だとされる / Noun + だとされる
explanation: Indicates a general belief or widely accepted view; 'is considered', 'is seen as', 'is said to be'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とされる grammar point expresses what is generally believed, accepted, or reputed. It can be translated as 'is considered to be,' 'is seen as,' or 'is said to be.' It’s often used in formal or written contexts. The structure differs slightly depending on whether it’s used with a noun, adjective, or verb.

title: ～としたところで
titleEn: Means 'even if' or 'even assuming'; emphasizes that the outcome won’t change.
structure: Verb-casual + としたところで / い-Adjective + としたところで / な-Adjective + だとしたところで / Noun + だとしたところで
structureEn: Verb-casual + としたところで / い-Adjective + としたところで / な-Adjective + だとしたところで / Noun + だとしたところで
explanation: Means 'even if' or 'even assuming'; emphasizes that the outcome won’t change. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～としたところで grammar point means 'even if' or 'even assuming that ~.' It highlights that even if the stated condition were met, the result would not significantly change. Often used to convey a sense of futility or skepticism in hypothetical statements.

title: ～とすると
titleEn: Expresses 'if', 'when', or 'assuming that' in a speculative sense.
structure: Verb-casual + とすると / い-Adjective + とすると / な-Adjective + だとすると / Noun + だとすると
structureEn: Verb-casual + とすると / い-Adjective + とすると / な-Adjective + だとすると / Noun + だとすると
explanation: Expresses 'if', 'when', or 'assuming that' in a speculative sense. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とすると grammar point is used for hypothetical or speculative situations, often in the sense of 'if we assume...', 'when it comes to...', or 'given that...' . It sets up a condition and then states the expected or possible outcome.
